passage.title: Riya's Big Dream
passage.text: Riya always wanted to become a doctor. As a child, she wanted to help sick people and make them healthy again. Her parents wanted her to study hard. Riya did not want to waste any time, so she studied every day. Her brother wanted to become an engineer, but he did not want to work long hours. One day, Riya's father asked, 'Did you want to become a doctor, or did someone pressure you?' Riya smiled and said, 'I just wanted to serve people — that is my true dream.' Her teacher always said she had the focus and dedication needed to succeed. Riya never stopped believing in what she wanted. Today, she is a successful doctor who still remembers what she wanted to achieve.
passage.hindiTranslation: Riya हमेशा से doctor बनना चाहती थी। बचपन में, वह बीमार लोगों की मदद करना और उन्हें ठीक करना चाहती थी। उसके माता-पिता चाहते थे कि वह मन लगाकर पढ़े। Riya किसी भी समय को बर्बाद नहीं करना चाहती थी, इसलिए वह रोज़ पढ़ती थी। उसका भाई engineer बनना चाहता था, लेकिन वह लंबे घंटे काम नहीं करना चाहता था। एक दिन Riya के पिता ने पूछा, 'क्या तुम doctor बनना चाहती थी, या किसी ने दबाव डाला?' Riya ने मुस्कुराते हुए कहा, 'मैं बस लोगों की सेवा करना चाहती थी — यही मेरा सच्चा सपना है।' उसकी teacher हमेशा कहती थी कि उसमें सफल होने के लिए ज़रूरी focus और dedication है। Riya ने कभी अपनी चाहत पर भरोसा करना नहीं छोड़ा। आज वह एक सफल doctor है जो अभी भी याद करती है कि वह क्या हासिल करना चाहती थी।

comprehensionQuestions.question: What did Riya always want to become?
comprehensionQuestions.explanation: The passage clearly states: 'Riya always wanted to become a doctor.'

comprehensionQuestions.question: What did Riya want to do for sick people?
options: Help them and make them healthy
comprehensionQuestions.answer: Help them and make them healthy
comprehensionQuestions.explanation: 'She wanted to help sick people and make them healthy again.'

comprehensionQuestions.question: What did Riya's parents want?
options: They wanted her to become an engineer.
They wanted her to study hard.
They wanted her to take rest.
They wanted her to travel abroad.
comprehensionQuestions.answer: They wanted her to study hard.
comprehensionQuestions.explanation: 'Her parents wanted her to study hard.' — 'wanted + object + to + verb' pattern.

comprehensionQuestions.question: What did Riya NOT want to do?
comprehensionQuestions.explanation: 'Riya did not want to waste any time' — past negative form.

comprehensionQuestions.question: What did Riya's brother want to become?
comprehensionQuestions.explanation: 'Her brother wanted to become an engineer.'

comprehensionQuestions.question: What did Riya's brother NOT want?
comprehensionQuestions.explanation: 'He did not want to work long hours.' — negative form of 'wanted to'.

comprehensionQuestions.question: What question did Riya's father ask her?
options: Did you want to study abroad?
Did you want to become a doctor, or did someone pressure you?
comprehensionQuestions.answer: Did you want to become a doctor, or did someone pressure you?
comprehensionQuestions.explanation: This is the direct question from the passage showing the question form of 'wanted'.

comprehensionQuestions.question: What did Riya say she just wanted to do?
comprehensionQuestions.explanation: 'I just wanted to serve people — that is my true dream.' — polite past expression.

comprehensionQuestions.question: Which sentence from the passage uses the 'Subject + wanted + object + to + verb' pattern?
options: Riya wanted to become a doctor.
Her parents wanted her to study hard.
She did not want to waste time.
Did you want to become a doctor?
comprehensionQuestions.answer: Her parents wanted her to study hard.
comprehensionQuestions.explanation: 'Parents wanted HER to study' = Subject + wanted + object + to + verb — advanced pattern.

comprehensionQuestions.question: What does the story suggest about Riya's determination?
options: She often gave up on her dreams.
She never stopped believing in what she wanted.
She wanted to change her career.
She wanted someone to help her.
comprehensionQuestions.answer: She never stopped believing in what she wanted.
comprehensionQuestions.explanation: 'Riya never stopped believing in what she wanted.' — directly stated in the passage.
